verdict_reasoning: ROR1 is explicitly described as a type I membrane protein and member of the ROR subfamily of cell surface receptors. Single-pass transmembrane architecture with an extracellular N-terminal domain and glycosylation support a classical surface-receptor topology. ROR1 functions as a co-receptor in non-canonical Wnt signaling and is a validated therapeutic target (CAR-T, ADC, and monoclonal antibody programs in B-cell lymphoid malignancies), confirming surface accessibility.
